projects.name: Calibrate
projects.short_description: An AI agent evaluation platform that helps non-profits test their entire agent stack
projects.description: Calibrate is a CLI and platform for evaluating AI agents, built for non-profits. It moves teams from slow, manual testing to a fast, automated, and repeatable process across the whole agent stack — LLM text-to-text quality and tool-calling, human-aligned LLM judges, speech-to-text and text-to-speech benchmarking across 10+ Indic languages, and realistic persona-driven simulations to surface failure modes. It is built on top of pipecat.

form.question: How to support
form.response: Star and contribute on GitHub: https://github.com/ARTPARK-SAHAI-ORG/calibrate — try it on your own agent stack, file issues, and help expand language and provider coverage.

form.question: A small brief about your project
form.response: Calibrate is an open-source AI agent evaluation platform for non-profits. It benchmarks LLMs, STT, and TTS providers, builds human-aligned LLM judges, and runs realistic conversation simulations so teams can ship reliable voice and text agents — with first-class support for Indic languages.

form.question: One FOSS maintainer lesson for your younger self
form.response: Ship early and let real users shape the roadmap. The feedback from someone actually depending on your tool is worth more than weeks of polishing in private.

form.question: Why do you do it? Why do you bother maintaining a FOSS project?
form.response: Building evaluation infrastructure in the open means non-profits with limited budgets can ship trustworthy AI for the communities they serve. Maintaining it openly is how that impact compounds beyond any single team.

form.question: If your repo had a theme song, what would it be?
form.response: Eye of the Tiger by Survivor

form.question: Which file in your project would you most like to set on fire?
form.response: The provider config glue code — every new STT/TTS integration leaves its fingerprints there.

form.question: What's your open-source villain origin story?
form.response: I kept re-running the same brittle, manual agent tests by hand and rage-built an automated evaluator so I'd never have to do it again.

form.question: If you had to use one emoji to convey what it is like to be a FOSS maintainer, what would it be?
